( ② ) The "strong" version, known as linguistic determinism, argues that language entirely determines our thought processes—we can only think in the categories provided by our language. ( ③ ) This deterministic view is now largely rejected by the scientific community as it is too rigid and lacks empirical support. ( ④ ) Instead, a "weak" version of the hypothesis, called linguistic relativity, has gained more acceptance. ( ⑤ ) For example, a language with many words for different types of snow may encourage its speakers to pay closer attention to those distinctions, but it does not prevent speakers of other languages from perceiving them.

vera's flavor 어휘

encapsulated 요약된    determinism 결정론    empirical 경험적인    nuanced 미묘한 차이가 있는

227 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 321-330
[ Passage 329 ]

329. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

The Protestant Reformation, the Scientific Revolution, and the Age of Enlightenment were all profoundly influenced by the rapid and widespread circulation of ideas that printing made possible.

The invention of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg in the mid-15th century was a pivotal event in human history, fundamentally altering the way information was created and disseminated. ( ① ) Before its invention, books were rare and expensive, meticulously copied by hand by scribes, a process that was both time-consuming and prone to error. ( ② ) The printing press allowed for the mass production of texts at a fraction of the cost. ( ③ ) Suddenly, books and pamphlets became available not just to the clergy and the nobility, but to a growing middle class and even some common people. ( ④ ) This newfound accessibility of knowledge empowered individuals to question traditional authorities and form their own opinions. ( ⑤ ) It laid the groundwork for the modern knowledge-based society.

vera's flavor 어휘

pivotal 중심축이 되는    meticulously 꼼꼼하게    clergy 성직자    groundwork 토대

[ Passage 330 ]

330. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

It could potentially justify sacrificing the rights or well-being of a small minority if doing so would produce a massive increase in the happiness of the majority.

Utilitarianism, a prominent ethical theory developed by philosophers like Jeremy Bentham and John Stuart Mill, proposes that the best moral action is the one that maximizes utility, usually defined as that which maximizes happiness or well-being for the greatest number of people. ( ① ) This principle, often summarized as "the greatest good for the greatest number," provides a straightforward framework for making ethical decisions. ( ② ) For example, when deciding on a public policy, a utilitarian would weigh the total benefits it brings to society against the total costs and harms. ( ③ ) If the overall happiness increases, the policy is considered morally right. ( ④ ) Such a calculation, however, raises a significant ethical problem. ( ⑤ ) This challenge highlights the tension between utilitarian calculations and the concept of inalienable individual rights.

vera's flavor 어휘

utilitarianism 공리주의    utility 효용    justify 정당화하다    inalienable 양도할 수 없는

228 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 331-340
[ Passage 331 ]

331. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

As a result, the body produces memory cells that 'remember' the specific pathogen.

Vaccines are a cornerstone of modern medicine, working by preparing the body's immune system to fight off future infections. The process begins with the introduction of a weakened or inactivated form of a pathogen, such as a virus or bacterium, into the body. ( ① ) This harmless version of the pathogen is known as an antigen. ( ② ) Upon detecting this antigen, the immune system is triggered to mount a response, just as it would against a real infection. ( ③ ) It produces antibodies, which are specialized proteins that can neutralize the invader. ( ④ ) These cells remain in the body for a long time, sometimes for a lifetime. ( ⑤ ) If the person is later exposed to the actual, harmful pathogen, these memory cells can quickly recognize it and launch a rapid and powerful immune response, preventing the disease from developing.

vera's flavor 어휘

cornerstone 초석    inactivated 비활성화된    antigen 항원    neutralize 무력화하다

[ Passage 332 ]

332. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Consequently, the harmful substances are converted into less toxic or non-toxic compounds like water and carbon dioxide.

Bioremediation is an innovative environmental technology that uses living organisms, primarily microorganisms, to clean up contaminated sites. This process leverages the natural metabolic pathways of microbes to break down pollutants. ( ① ) When a site, such as soil or groundwater, is contaminated with substances like oil or pesticides, specific microorganisms that can feed on these contaminants are introduced or stimulated. ( ② ) These microbes essentially "eat" the pollutants, using them as a source of food and energy. ( ③ ) This transformation is the core of the cleanup process, effectively neutralizing the threat to the ecosystem. ( ④ ) The effectiveness of bioremediation depends on various factors, including temperature, nutrient availability, and the type of contaminant. ( ⑤ ) It is considered a green solution because it relies on natural processes and minimizes the need for harsh chemicals.

vera's flavor 어휘

bioremediation 생물학적 정화    leverages 활용하다    metabolic 신진대사의    contaminants 오염물질

229 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 331-340
[ Passage 333 ]

333. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This structure makes the system incredibly resilient to tampering or failure.

Blockchain technology is most famous for powering cryptocurrencies, but its core principles have far-reaching implications. The defining feature of blockchain is its decentralized nature. ( ① ) Unlike traditional databases that are stored in a central location, a blockchain is a distributed ledger, meaning an identical copy of the transaction record is held by numerous computers across a network. ( ② ) Whenever a new transaction occurs, it is verified by the network participants and added as a new "block" to the "chain." ( ③ ) As a result, no single entity has control over the entire network, which enhances security and transparency. ( ④ ) If a malicious actor wanted to alter a transaction, they would need to alter the block on a majority of the computers in the network simultaneously, which is practically impossible. ( ⑤ ) This characteristic makes blockchain a promising technology for applications beyond finance, including supply chain management and voting systems.

vera's flavor 어휘

decentralized 분산된    ledger 원장    resilient 회복력 있는    tampering 간섭, 조작

[ Passage 334 ]

334. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This feeling of unity, often called "communitas," helps to maintain social order and cohesion.

Rituals are a fundamental aspect of human culture, found in every known society. They are structured sequences of actions and words that are performed in a specific order. ( ① ) While the content of rituals varies widely—from religious ceremonies to national holidays—their social function often remains consistent. ( ② ) One of the primary functions is to bring people together and create a sense of community. ( ③ ) During a ritual, individuals set aside their personal concerns and focus on a common purpose or belief, acting in a synchronized manner. ( ④ ) Consequently, these shared experiences strengthen the bonds among participants and reinforce a sense of collective identity. ( ⑤ ) Therefore, rituals are not just empty traditions; they are powerful tools for building and sustaining social groups.

vera's flavor 어휘

rituals 의식    consistent 일관된    synchronized 동조화된    cohesion 응집력

230 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 331-340
[ Passage 335 ]

335. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

The guidance acts as a scaffold, providing just enough support to help the learner bridge the gap on their own.

The educational psychologist Lev Vygotsky introduced the concept of the Zone of Proximal Development (ZPD) to describe the sweet spot for learning. The ZPD is the gap between what a learner can do independently and what they can achieve with guidance from a more knowledgeable person. ( ① ) This guide could be a teacher, a peer, or even a parent. ( ② ) Vygotsky's theory posits that learning is most effective when it occurs within this zone. ( ③ ) For instance, a child might not be able to solve a complex math problem alone, but they can solve it with targeted hints from a teacher. ( ④ ) As a result, the learner can successfully perform the task and internalize the new skill, effectively expanding their independent capabilities. ( ⑤ ) This process is not about simply being given the answer, but about being guided to discover it.

vera's flavor 어휘

proximal 근접한    scaffold 비계, 발판    internalize 내면화하다    capabilities 능력

[ Passage 336 ]

336. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

These energy-carrying molecules then power the second stage of photosynthesis, the Calvin cycle.

Photosynthesis is the vital process by which plants, algae, and some bacteria convert light energy into chemical energy. This complex process can be divided into two main stages. ( ① ) The first stage is known as the light-dependent reactions, which take place in the thylakoid membranes of chloroplasts. ( ② ) During this stage, chlorophyll and other pigments absorb energy from sunlight. ( ③ ) This captured light energy is used to split water molecules, releasing oxygen as a byproduct. ( ④ ) Consequently, the energy captured from sunlight is stored in the chemical bonds of molecules like ATP and NADPH. ( ⑤ ) In the Calvin cycle, this stored chemical energy is used to convert carbon dioxide from the atmosphere into glucose, a sugar that provides energy for the plant's growth and development.

vera's flavor 어휘

chloroplasts 엽록체    pigments 색소    byproduct 부산물    glucose 포도당

231 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 331-340
[ Passage 337 ]

337. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

For example, limestone, a sedimentary rock, can be metamorphosed into marble, and shale can be transformed into slate.

The Earth's crust is in a constant state of change, leading to the formation of three main types of rocks: igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic. Metamorphic rocks are formed from the transformation of existing rock types in a process called metamorphism. ( ① ) This process occurs when pre-existing rocks are subjected to intense heat, high pressure, or chemical reactions deep within the Earth. ( ② ) The conditions for metamorphism are typically found where tectonic plates collide or where magma intrudes into the surrounding rock. ( ③ ) As a result, the original rock's mineral composition and texture are fundamentally altered, creating a new type of rock. ( ④ ) The degree of change depends on the intensity of the heat and pressure applied. ( ⑤ ) This transformation does not melt the rock, but rather recrystallizes it, distinguishing metamorphic rocks from igneous rocks, which form from molten material.

vera's flavor 어휘

metamorphic 변성의    subjected to ~을 받는    intrudes 침입하다    recrystallizes 재결정하다

[ Passage 338 ]

338. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This price reduction continues until the surplus is eliminated and the market returns to equilibrium.

The law of supply and demand is a fundamental concept in economics that explains how prices are determined in a market economy. It states that the price of a good will adjust to the point where the quantity demanded by consumers equals the quantity supplied by producers. ( ① ) This point is known as the equilibrium price. ( ② ) However, markets are not always in equilibrium. ( ③ ) When the quantity supplied of a good exceeds the quantity demanded at a certain price, a situation known as a surplus occurs. ( ④ ) Consequently, producers are often forced to lower their prices to sell off the excess inventory and attract more buyers. ( ⑤ ) Conversely, if demand exceeds supply, a shortage occurs, which tends to drive prices up.

vera's flavor 어휘

equilibrium 평형, 균형    surplus 과잉    inventory 재고    shortage 부족

232 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 331-340
[ Passage 339 ]

339. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

At this point, the neutral stimulus (the bell) has become a conditioned stimulus, and the salivation in response to the bell is a conditioned response.

Classical conditioning is a learning process first demonstrated by the Russian physiologist Ivan Pavlov. His famous experiments with dogs revealed how associations between stimuli can lead to new behaviors. ( ① ) In his setup, the presentation of food (an unconditioned stimulus) naturally caused the dogs to salivate (an unconditioned response). ( ② ) Pavlov then began to ring a bell (a neutral stimulus) just before presenting the food. ( ③ ) He repeated this pairing of the bell and the food multiple times. ( ④ ) As a result, the neutral stimulus alone begins to elicit the response that was originally triggered only by the unconditioned stimulus. ( ⑤ ) This principle explains how many involuntary responses, such as fears and phobias, can be learned.

vera's flavor 어휘

conditioning 조건 형성    stimulus 자극    salivate 침을 흘리다    elicit 이끌어내다

[ Passage 340 ]

340. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This principle is vital for many biological functions, such as how plant roots absorb water from the soil and how kidneys regulate water balance in the human body.

Osmosis is a crucial biological process that involves the movement of water across a semipermeable membrane. This type of membrane allows small molecules like water to pass through but blocks larger molecules like salt or sugar (solutes). ( ① ) The direction of water movement is determined by the concentration of solutes on either side of the membrane. ( ② ) Specifically, water will always move from an area of lower solute concentration to an area of higher solute concentration. ( ③ ) This net movement of water continues until the concentrations reach a state of balance or equilibrium. ( ④ ) Consequently, the concentration of the solute becomes more equal on both sides of the membrane. ( ⑤ ) Without osmosis, cells would not be able to maintain their internal environment and would quickly perish.

vera's flavor 어휘

osmosis 삼투    semipermeable 반투과성의    solutes 용질    perish 소멸하다

233 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 341-350
[ Passage 341 ]

341. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Instead, the crust buckled and folded, thrusting rock upwards to create the world's highest mountain range.

The formation of the Himalayas is a dramatic story of geological change, a direct result of plate tectonics. Millions of years ago, the landmass that is now the Indian subcontinent was an island moving steadily northward across the Tethys Ocean. ( ① ) This journey culminated in a colossal collision with the Eurasian Plate. ( ② ) The immense pressure from this impact had to be released. ( ③ ) In addition, because both were continental plates of similar density, neither could be subducted under the other. ( ④ ) This process is not over; the Indian Plate continues to push against the Eurasian Plate, causing the Himalayas to rise by a few millimeters each year. ( ⑤ ) This ongoing tectonic activity is also the reason for the region's frequent and powerful earthquakes.

vera's flavor 어휘

culminated in ~으로 절정에 달했다    colossal 거대한    subducted 섭입된    tectonic 지각 구조의

[ Passage 342 ]

342. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

These armored vessels were far more resilient to enemy fire.

The mid-19th century witnessed a radical transformation in naval warfare, primarily driven by technological advancements. For centuries, wooden sailing ships had dominated the seas, but their vulnerability was starkly exposed by the invention of explosive naval shells. ( ① ) These shells could easily tear through wooden hulls, rendering traditional warships obsolete. ( ② ) In response, naval engineers began cladding ships in thick iron plates, creating the first "ironclads." ( ③ ) Furthermore, the adoption of steam engines freed these new warships from the unpredictability of the wind, granting them unprecedented tactical mobility. ( ④ ) The Battle of Hampton Roads during the American Civil War famously demonstrated the superiority of ironclads over their wooden counterparts, marking a definitive end to the age of sail in military contexts. ( ⑤ ) This combination of armor and independent propulsion defined the next generation of naval power.

vera's flavor 어휘

naval warfare 해전    obsolete 구식의    cladding 외장을 입히는    resilient 회복력 있는

234 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 341-350
[ Passage 343 ]

343. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This flexibility means that exchange rates can be volatile, introducing uncertainty into international commerce.

For nearly three decades after World War II, the global economy operated under the Bretton Woods system, which pegged major currencies to the U.S. dollar, itself convertible to gold at a fixed rate. This created a stable, predictable environment for international trade. ( ① ) However, by the early 1970s, economic pressures, particularly from U.S. spending on the Vietnam War and domestic programs, made the system unsustainable. ( ② ) In 1971, the United States unilaterally terminated the convertibility of the dollar to gold, effectively collapsing the Bretton Woods agreement. ( ③ ) This led to the modern era of floating exchange rates, where currency values are determined by supply and demand in the foreign exchange market. ( ④ ) In addition, this new system allowed countries to use monetary policy more freely to address domestic economic issues like unemployment or inflation. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

pegged 고정된    unsustainable 지속 불가능한    unilaterally 일방적으로    volatile 변동성이 큰

[ Passage 344 ]

344. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This pursuit of realism extended to the depiction of the world itself.

The transition from medieval art to the Renaissance marked one of the most significant shifts in Western cultural history. Medieval art was predominantly religious in theme and symbolic in nature, often depicting figures in a flat, stylized manner without a strong sense of realism. ( ① ) The primary goal was to convey spiritual truths rather than to represent the natural world accurately. ( ② ) With the dawn of the Renaissance, however, a renewed interest in classical humanism led to a profound change in artistic focus. ( ③ ) Artists started to celebrate the human form, studying anatomy to portray figures with lifelike accuracy and emotional depth. ( ④ ) Furthermore, artists began to employ new techniques like linear perspective to create a convincing illusion of three-dimensional space on a two-dimensional surface. ( ⑤ ) This combination of humanistic subjects and realistic techniques defined the art of the era.

vera's flavor 어휘

predominantly 주로    stylized 양식화된    anatomy 해부학    linear perspective 선형 원근법

235 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 341-350
[ Passage 345 ]

345. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Information can become trapped within isolated clusters or "echo chambers" if the network lacks bridges between different communities.

The proliferation of social network services (SNS) has fundamentally altered the dynamics of information diffusion. In traditional media, information flowed in a top-down manner, from a few sources to a large audience. ( ① ) On SNS, however, any user can be a source, leading to a more decentralized and complex pattern of dissemination. ( ② ) The virality of a piece of content often depends on its perceived novelty, emotional resonance, and the credibility of the initial sharer. ( ③ ) A message originating from a highly trusted individual is more likely to be accepted and passed on. ( ④ ) In addition, the network structure itself—who is connected to whom—plays a critical role in how widely and quickly information spreads. ( ⑤ ) Understanding these factors is crucial for anyone trying to communicate effectively in the digital age.

vera's flavor 어휘

proliferation 확산    diffusion 보급, 확산    dissemination 보급    virality 바이럴, 입소문

[ Passage 346 ]

346. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This influx of knowledge contributed to the intellectual ferment that would eventually blossom into the Renaissance.

The Crusades, a series of religious wars between the 11th and 13th centuries, are often remembered for their military conflicts. However, their long-term impact on European society extended far beyond the battlefield. ( ① ) One of the most significant consequences was the expansion of trade between Europe and the Middle East. ( ② ) Italian city-states like Venice and Genoa grew wealthy by transporting crusaders and establishing new trade routes for spices, silks, and other luxury goods. ( ③ ) This commercial revival helped to weaken the feudal system, as a new merchant class emerged and the economy shifted from being land-based to more reliant on cash. ( ④ ) Furthermore, the returning Crusaders brought back new goods, scientific knowledge, and philosophical ideas from the more advanced Islamic world, stimulating a cultural and intellectual awakening in Europe. ( ⑤ ) Thus, the Crusades inadvertently helped to lay the groundwork for modern Europe.

vera's flavor 어휘

Crusades 십자군 전쟁    revival 부흥    ferment 동요, 발효    inadvertently 의도치 않게

236 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 341-350
[ Passage 347 ]

347. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This marked a clear departure from the Impressionist goal of objective observation.

The evolution from Impressionism to Post-Impressionism is clearly visible in the career of Vincent van Gogh. Initially, his work was influenced by the Impressionists' focus on capturing the fleeting effects of light and atmosphere. ( ① ) Like them, he painted outdoors and depicted everyday scenes. ( ② ) However, van Gogh soon grew dissatisfied with what he saw as the Impressionists' superficial concern for visual reality. ( ③ ) He sought to imbue his art with deeper personal meaning and psychological intensity. ( ④ ) In addition, he began to use thick, visible brushstrokes and vivid, often unnatural colors to convey his emotional response to a subject, rather than its objective appearance. ( ⑤ ) His famous work, "The Starry Night," for example, is less a depiction of a night sky and more an expression of his turbulent inner world.

vera's flavor 어휘

fleeting 덧없는    superficial 피상적인    imbue 불어넣다    turbulent 격동의

[ Passage 348 ]

348. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

It explained that the movement was driven by convection currents within the mantle.

The theory of plate tectonics, developed in the mid-20th century, revolutionized our understanding of the Earth. It built upon the earlier idea of continental drift, proposed by Alfred Wegener in 1912. ( ① ) Wegener had amassed considerable evidence—such as the jigsaw-puzzle fit of continents and fossil similarities across oceans—but he could not explain the force that moved the continents. ( ② ) His theory was therefore largely dismissed by the scientific community for decades. ( ③ ) Plate tectonics filled this crucial gap by proposing that the Earth's outer shell is divided into several large plates that move over the molten upper layer of the mantle. ( ④ ) Furthermore, the theory provided a unifying mechanism for phenomena that had previously seemed unrelated, such as earthquakes, volcanic activity, and the formation of mountain ranges. ( ⑤ ) This comprehensive explanatory power is why plate tectonics is now considered the cornerstone of modern geology.

vera's flavor 어휘

amassed 축적했다    dismissed 묵살된    convection currents 대류    cornerstone 초석

237 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 341-350
[ Passage 349 ]

349. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This dynamic illustrates the complex trade-offs governments and central banks face when managing their currency's value.

The fluctuation of a country's currency exchange rate has profound effects on its international trade and domestic economy. When a country's currency appreciates, or strengthens, against foreign currencies, it means that one unit of the domestic currency can buy more foreign currency. ( ① ) This makes imported goods cheaper for domestic consumers, which can help to control inflation. ( ② ) However, this same strength can be a double-edged sword. ( ③ ) For foreign buyers, a stronger domestic currency means that the country's export products become more expensive, which can lead to a decrease in export volume. ( ④ ) Conversely, when a currency depreciates, or weakens, imports become more expensive for domestic consumers. ( ⑤ ) In addition, a weaker domestic currency makes a country's exports cheaper and more competitive on the global market, potentially boosting sales and production.

vera's flavor 어휘

fluctuation 변동    appreciates 가치가 오르다    double-edged sword 양날의 검    depreciates 가치가 떨어지다

[ Passage 350 ]

350. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This ability to precisely document and share complex musical ideas laid the foundation for the entire subsequent history of Western classical music.

The evolution of Western music was profoundly shaped by the development of a standardized system of musical notation during the Middle Ages. Before notation, music was transmitted orally, a process prone to error and change over generations. ( ① ) A piece of music could exist in countless variations, and complex compositions were difficult to preserve and disseminate accurately. ( ② ) The invention of a staff-based system, largely credited to Guido of Arezzo in the 11th century, was a revolutionary step. ( ③ ) It allowed for the precise recording of pitch, and later, rhythm. ( ④ ) Furthermore, the development of notation allowed composers to write down complex polyphonic music, where multiple independent melodic lines are interwoven. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

notation 기보법    orally 구두로    polyphonic 다성의    interwoven 뒤섞인

238 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 351-360
[ Passage 351 ]

351. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Without this, some students may become "free-riders," relying on the efforts of others without contributing themselves.

Cooperative learning, where students work together in small groups, is often praised as a powerful educational strategy. However, its effectiveness is not automatic. ( ① ) For cooperative learning to be truly successful, several key conditions must be met. ( ② ) First, there must be "positive interdependence," a sense that the group sinks or swims together. ( ③ ) Each member's effort is required for the group's success. ( ④ ) Second, "individual accountability" is crucial; every student must be responsible for their own learning and for contributing to the group. ( ⑤ ) Finally, clear goals and well-defined roles within the group help to structure the interaction and prevent conflict.

vera's flavor 어휘

cooperative 협동의    interdependence 상호의존    accountability 책무성    free-riders 무임승차자

[ Passage 352 ]

352. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This principle grants individuals the right, under certain conditions, to request that search engines and other data controllers remove links to personal information about them.

In the digital age, our personal information is more accessible and persistent than ever before. Every search, purchase, and social media post contributes to a vast digital footprint that can be difficult to manage or erase. ( ① ) This permanence raises significant privacy concerns, as past mistakes or irrelevant information can haunt individuals indefinitely. ( ② ) In response to this problem, the concept of the "right to be forgotten" has emerged, particularly in European law. ( ③ ) The aim is not to rewrite history but to allow people to move on from past events that are no longer relevant, ensuring that their digital identity does not perpetually define their present and future. ( ④ ) This right represents a crucial step in rebalancing the power between individuals and data giants. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

persistent 지속적인    footprint 발자국    permanence 영속성    perpetually 영구적으로

239 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 351-360
[ Passage 353 ]

353. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

The goal was not to depict the external world logically but to explore the inner world of the psyche, with all its strange and unsettling imagery.

Surrealism, an influential art movement of the early 20th century, sought to unlock the power of the unconscious mind. ( ① ) Deeply influenced by Sigmund Freud's theories on dreams and the subconscious, Surrealist artists aimed to bypass rational thought and tap into a deeper, more authentic reality. ( ② ) They used techniques like "automatism," which involved automatic drawing or writing without conscious control, to reveal hidden thoughts and desires. ( ③ ) Painters like Salvador Dalí created bizarre, dreamlike landscapes where everyday objects were distorted and placed in unexpected contexts. ( ④ ) This exploration of the irrational was a radical departure from traditional art, which had long prioritized reason and order. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

Surrealism 초현실주의    unconscious 무의식    automatism 자동기술법    psyche 정신

[ Passage 354 ]

354. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This puzzle has been debated by philosophers for centuries, exploring concepts of identity, persistence, and change.

The Ship of Theseus is a thought experiment that raises the question of whether an object that has had all of its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object. ( ① ) The paradox is most notably recorded by the ancient Greek historian Plutarch. ( ② ) In his account, the ship that the hero Theseus sailed on was preserved for a long time by the Athenians. ( ③ ) He documented this gradual replacement of the ship's planks over time, noting that the ship was repaired bit by bit until no original timber remained. ( ④ ) The core philosophical question then arises: is the restored ship the same as the original one? ( ⑤ ) It forces us to consider what constitutes identity—is it the physical material, the form, or something else entirely?

vera's flavor 어휘

paradox 역설    components 구성 요소    persistence 지속성    constitutes 구성하다

240 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 351-360
[ Passage 355 ]

355. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

However, this strong view is not widely accepted today.

The Sapir-Whorf hypothesis, also known as linguistic relativity, is a concept in linguistics and cognitive science. ( ① ) It posits that there is a systematic relationship between the grammatical categories of the language a person speaks and how that person both understands the world and behaves in it. ( ② ) This principle suggests that the structure of a language affects its speakers' worldview or cognition. ( ③ ) For example, some languages have many different words for snow, which might allow their speakers to perceive and think about snow in ways that speakers of English cannot. ( ④ ) The stronger version of the hypothesis, linguistic determinism, claims that language entirely determines thought. ( ⑤ ) Most contemporary linguists support a weaker version, which allows for the influence of language on thought without claiming it is completely determined.

vera's flavor 어휘

linguistic relativity 언어 상대성    cognition 인지    determinism 결정론    contemporary 현대의

[ Passage 356 ]

356. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This, in turn, can lead to an atmosphere of anarchy, making residents feel fearful and attracting more serious criminal activity.

The "broken windows" theory is a criminological theory that suggests visible signs of crime, anti-social behavior, and civil disorder create an urban environment that encourages further crime and disorder, including serious crimes. ( ① ) The theory was introduced in 1982 by social scientists James Q. Wilson and George L. Kelling. ( ② ) It states that maintaining and monitoring urban environments in a well-ordered condition may stop further vandalism and escalation into more serious crime. ( ③ ) For instance, a single broken window, if left unrepaired, invites more windows to be broken. ( ④ ) They argue that this sends a signal to potential criminals that the community is disordered and that no one is in control. ( ⑤ ) Therefore, proponents of this theory advocate for a police strategy that targets minor offenses to prevent major ones.

vera's flavor 어휘

criminological 범죄학의    vandalism 공공 기물 파손    anarchy 무정부 상태    proponents 지지자들

241 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 351-360
[ Passage 357 ]

357. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

It helped to move painting away from the flat, linear style of the Middle Ages toward a more dynamic and lifelike representation.

Chiaroscuro is a term in art for the use of strong contrasts between light and dark, usually bold contrasts affecting a whole composition. ( ① ) It is a technical term used by artists and art historians for the use of contrasts of light to achieve a sense of volume in modeling three-dimensional objects and figures. ( ② ) While it became a prominent feature of Renaissance art, its origins can be traced back to ancient Greek and Roman painting. ( ③ ) Artists like Leonardo da Vinci and Caravaggio are renowned for their masterful use of chiaroscuro. ( ④ ) By illuminating figures against a dark background, they could model their subjects with a new level of realism and emotional intensity. ( ⑤ ) This technique was used to create a dramatic sense of volume and three-dimensionality on a flat surface.

vera's flavor 어휘

chiaroscuro 명암대조법    prominent 저명한, 두드러진    renowned 유명한    illuminating 비추는

[ Passage 358 ]

358. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

He argued that in such a situation, individual users acting independently according to their own self-interest behave contrary to the common good of all users.

The tragedy of the commons is an economic and social dilemma described by ecologist Garrett Hardin in 1968. ( ① ) It refers to a situation where a shared, limited resource becomes depleted due to individuals acting in their own self-interest. ( ② ) Hardin used the example of a pasture open to all local herders. ( ③ ) Each herder is motivated to add more cattle to their herd, as they receive the full benefit from their own animals. ( ④ ) However, the cost of overgrazing is shared by all herders, leading to the eventual destruction of the pasture. ( ⑤ ) This concept is now widely used to explain problems like overfishing, deforestation, and air pollution.

vera's flavor 어휘

dilemma 딜레마    depleted 고갈된    contrary to ~에 반하여    common good 공공선

242 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 351-360
[ Passage 359 ]

359. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Think of the evolution from early room-sized computers to the powerful smartphones we carry in our pockets today.

Moore's Law is the observation made by Intel co-founder Gordon Moore in 1965. ( ① ) He noted that the number of transistors on a microchip doubles approximately every two years, though the cost of computers is halved. ( ② ) This observation is not a physical or natural law but rather a projection of a historical trend. ( ③ ) For decades, the semiconductor industry has used it as a guide for long-term planning and to set targets for research and development. ( ④ ) It has been a primary driver of the digital revolution, enabling the creation of more powerful and affordable electronic devices. ( ⑤ ) While some experts believe its pace is slowing, the principle of exponential growth it represents continues to inspire innovation.

vera's flavor 어휘

transistors 트랜지스터    projection 예측, 투영    semiconductor 반도체    exponential 기하급수적인

[ Passage 360 ]

360. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This example illustrates how a single species at the top can indirectly engineer the entire landscape.

A trophic cascade is an ecological phenomenon triggered by the addition or removal of top predators in a food web. ( ① ) These changes in the predator population result in reciprocal changes in the relative populations of predator and prey through a food chain. ( ② ) They can have dramatic and unforeseen consequences on the ecosystem's structure and function. ( ③ ) A classic example occurred in Yellowstone National Park after wolves were reintroduced in 1995. ( ④ ) The wolves preyed on elk, causing a decrease in the elk population. ( ⑤ ) With fewer elk grazing, willow and aspen trees began to recover, which in turn provided better habitats for beavers and songbirds.

vera's flavor 어휘

trophic cascade 영양 폭포    reciprocal 상호간의    unforeseen 예측하지 못한    engineer 만들다, 설계하다

243 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 361-370
[ Passage 361 ]

361. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Based on this error value, the network then adjusts the connection weights between its neurons, working backward from the output layer to the input layer.

An artificial neural network, the foundational structure of deep learning, learns to perform tasks by processing vast amounts of data. The learning process, often called training, is iterative. ( ① ) During each iteration, the network takes an input, such as an image, and makes a prediction about it. ( ② ) This prediction is then compared to the correct, human-provided label, and the difference between the two is calculated as an "error" or "loss." ( ③ ) This crucial step is known as backpropagation and is the core mechanism by which the network learns. ( ④ ) Essentially, it is a method of assigning responsibility for the error to each neuron and adjusting its contribution. ( ⑤ ) This cycle of prediction, error calculation, and weight adjustment is repeated millions of times until the network's predictions become consistently accurate.

vera's flavor 어휘

foundational 기초가 되는    iterative 반복적인    backpropagation 역전파    contribution 기여

[ Passage 362 ]

362. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This principle is explicitly echoed in the Declaration of Independence.

The American Revolution was not merely a war for independence; it was also an intellectual revolution deeply rooted in the ideas of the Enlightenment. Thinkers of this era championed concepts like natural rights, liberty, and the social contract. ( ① ) John Locke, an English philosopher, was particularly influential. ( ② ) His theory that governments are instituted to protect the rights to life, liberty, and property provided the colonists with a powerful justification for rebelling against British rule. ( ③ ) Similarly, the writings of Montesquieu, particularly his advocacy for the separation of powers, profoundly shaped the structure of the new American government. ( ④ ) The framers of the U.S. Constitution drew upon this idea to create a system of checks and balances among the legislative, executive, and judicial branches. ( ⑤ ) Thus, the foundational documents of the United States are a testament to the enduring legacy of Enlightenment philosophy.

vera's flavor 어휘

rooted in ~에 뿌리를 둔    justification 정당화    explicitly 명시적으로    testament 증거

244 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 361-370
[ Passage 363 ]

363. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

As a result, the market overproduces the good because the producers do not face the full cost of their actions, leading to an inefficient allocation of resources.

In economics, an externality is a cost or benefit caused by a producer that is not financially incurred or received by that producer. A negative externality occurs when the production or consumption of a product results in a cost to a third party. ( ① ) Consider a factory that pollutes a river. ( ② ) The cost of this pollution—such as harm to fish stocks, reduced water quality for downstream communities, and health problems—is borne by society, not by the factory. ( ③ ) The factory's private cost of production includes labor, materials, and energy, but it does not include the cost of the environmental damage it creates. ( ④ ) This discrepancy means the true cost of production to society is not reflected in the market price of the goods. ( ⑤ ) Governments may try to correct this market failure through taxes, regulations, or tradable permits.

vera's flavor 어휘

externality 외부효과    incurred 발생된    discrepancy 불일치    allocation 배분

[ Passage 364 ]

364. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This gap creates numerous social problems, including issues of cyberbullying, misinformation, and the exploitation of personal data.

The concept of "cultural lag," introduced by sociologist William F. Ogburn, describes a situation where the material culture of a society changes more rapidly than its non-material culture. Material culture includes technology, inventions, and physical objects, while non-material culture encompasses values, beliefs, norms, and social institutions. ( ① ) A classic example of this phenomenon can be seen in the development of the internet and digital communication technologies. ( ② ) These technologies evolved at a breathtaking pace, fundamentally altering how people interact, work, and access information. ( ③ ) However, the non-material culture, such as laws regarding online privacy, ethical guidelines for data usage, and social etiquette for digital interactions, has developed much more slowly. ( ④ ) Consequently, a period of maladjustment arises where established social norms and moral codes struggle to adapt to the new material conditions. ( ⑤ ) Navigating this lag requires conscious effort from individuals and societies to create new frameworks that align with technological realities.

vera's flavor 어휘

cultural lag 문화 지체    encompasses 포함하다    breathtaking 숨 막히는    maladjustment 부조정

245 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 361-370
[ Passage 365 ]

365. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This continuous strand is known as the leading strand, and the fragmented one is called the lagging strand.

DNA replication is the biological process of producing two identical replicas of DNA from one original DNA molecule. This process is fundamental for cell division and the transmission of genetic information. It begins when an enzyme called helicase unwinds the DNA double helix, separating it into two single strands. ( ① ) These separated strands then serve as templates for creating new complementary strands. ( ② ) An enzyme named DNA polymerase moves along each template strand, adding matching nucleotides to build the new DNA molecule. ( ③ ) However, because DNA polymerase can only synthesize DNA in one direction, one of the new strands is made continuously, while the other is synthesized in small, discontinuous pieces. ( ④ ) Another enzyme, DNA ligase, then joins these small pieces, known as Okazaki fragments, into a single, unified strand. ( ⑤ ) The result is two new DNA molecules, each consisting of one original and one newly synthesized strand.

vera's flavor 어휘

replication 복제    unwinds 풀다    nucleotides 뉴클레오타이드    discontinuous 불연속적인

[ Passage 366 ]

366. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This shift in artistic goals gave rise to Post-Impressionism, a diverse movement that included figures such as Paul Cézanne, Vincent van Gogh, and Paul Gauguin.

Impressionism, which emerged in the late 19th century, marked a radical departure from traditional academic painting. Artists like Claude Monet and Pierre-Auguste Renoir focused on capturing the fleeting effects of light and atmosphere. ( ① ) They often painted outdoors, using rapid, visible brushstrokes to convey the immediate sensation of a particular moment. ( ② ) The subject matter was typically modern life, landscapes, and scenes of leisure, rendered without idealized perfection. ( ③ ) However, by the 1880s, a new generation of artists began to feel that the Impressionist focus on optical reality was too restrictive. ( ④ ) They sought instead to imbue their work with more solid, durable structure and a greater degree of personal, emotional expression. ( ⑤ ) While these artists built upon the Impressionists' use of vivid color, they each developed a unique style that paved the way for modern art.

vera's flavor 어휘

fleeting 덧없는    rendered 표현된    restrictive 제한적인    imbue 불어넣다

246 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 361-370
[ Passage 367 ]

367. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

As a result, organisms at the top of the food chain can carry toxin concentrations millions of times higher than those found in the environment.

Biomagnification, also known as bioamplification, is the process by which the concentration of a persistent toxin increases in the tissues of organisms at successively higher levels in a food chain. This phenomenon occurs because the toxin is absorbed or ingested by an organism and is not easily broken down or excreted. ( ① ) When this organism is eaten by another, the toxin is transferred to the predator. ( ② ) Because the predator consumes many contaminated prey over its lifetime, the toxin accumulates to a much higher concentration in its body. ( ③ ) This process repeats at each trophic level. ( ④ ) For instance, a top predator like an eagle might consume fish that have eaten smaller organisms, accumulating toxins from every level of the food web below it. ( ⑤ ) This can lead to severe health issues, reproductive failure, and even death in these apex species.

vera's flavor 어휘

biomagnification 생물 농축    persistent 지속적인    excreted 배설된    trophic level 영양 단계

[ Passage 368 ]

368. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This more moderate perspective is the "weak" version, or linguistic relativity, which proposes that language merely influences, rather than governs, our thinking and perception.

The Sapir-Whorf hypothesis posits a compelling relationship between the language a person speaks and how that person understands the world. It exists in two forms: a "strong" version and a "weak" version. ( ① ) The strong version, known as linguistic determinism, argues that language dictates thought and that linguistic categories limit and determine cognitive categories. ( ② ) According to this view, our reality is fundamentally shaped by the grammatical and lexical structures of our native tongue. ( ③ ) This does not mean, however, that speakers of a language lacking a specific word for a color are incapable of perceiving that color. ( ④ ) Rather, it suggests they may find it more difficult to categorize or remember it, highlighting a subtle influence rather than an insurmountable barrier. ( ⑤ ) Most contemporary linguists and cognitive scientists support this weaker form of the hypothesis.

vera's flavor 어휘

posits 상정하다    lexical 어휘의    insurmountable 극복할 수 없는    governs 지배하다

247 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 361-370
[ Passage 369 ]

369. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

The universe itself is often considered the ultimate isolated system, leading to the conclusion that its total entropy is constantly increasing.

The second law of thermodynamics is a fundamental principle of physics with profound implications. It is often stated in terms of entropy, which can be understood as a measure of disorder or randomness in a system. ( ① ) The law asserts that the total entropy of an isolated system can only increase over time; it can never decrease. ( ② ) An isolated system is defined as one that cannot exchange either energy or matter with its surroundings. ( ③ ) This principle explains why certain processes occur spontaneously while others do not. ( ④ ) For example, heat naturally flows from a hot object to a cold one, increasing the overall disorder, but the reverse never happens on its own. ( ⑤ ) In the real world, truly isolated systems are rare, but the concept is crucial for theoretical understanding.

vera's flavor 어휘

thermodynamics 열역학    entropy 엔트로피    isolated 고립된    spontaneously 자발적으로

[ Passage 370 ]

370. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

When there is an inconsistency, something must change to eliminate the dissonance.

Cognitive dissonance is a psychological theory that refers to the mental stress experienced by a person who holds two or more contradictory beliefs, ideas, or values, or performs an action that is contradictory to one of these. ( ① ) This state of internal inconsistency causes a feeling of discomfort. ( ② ) To alleviate this mental discomfort, the individual is motivated to change one of these elements to restore balance. ( ③ ) For example, a person who smokes cigarettes (behavior) while knowing that smoking causes cancer (cognition) will experience cognitive dissonance. ( ④ ) They might decide to quit smoking, dismiss the evidence about cancer, or rationalize their behavior by saying they only smoke socially. ( ⑤ ) The theory, proposed by Leon Festinger, suggests that people have a fundamental drive to seek consistency among their cognitions.

vera's flavor 어휘

contradictory 모순되는    alleviate 완화하다    rationalize 합리화하다    cognitions 인지

248 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 371-380
[ Passage 371 ]

371. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This is because the very skills required to perform a task well are often the same skills needed to recognize one's own deficiencies.

The Dunning-Kruger effect is a cognitive bias in which people with low ability in a specific area tend to overestimate their competence. ( ① ) This paradox occurs because incompetent individuals lack the metacognitive ability to realize how poorly they are performing. ( ② ) For instance, a novice musician might believe their performance was flawless, unable to perceive the subtle errors in timing and pitch that an expert would immediately notice. ( ③ ) Conversely, the effect also describes a related bias where highly competent individuals may underestimate their relative ability. ( ④ ) They might mistakenly assume that tasks which are easy for them are also easy for others, leading them to misjudge their own level of expertise. ( ⑤ ) This dual-faceted bias highlights the complex relationship between self-perception and actual ability.

vera's flavor 어휘

overestimate 과대평가하다    competence 능력    metacognitive 초인지의    deficiencies 결함

[ Passage 372 ]

372. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This process of internalization is crucial for the development of higher cognitive functions, such as problem-solving and self-regulation.

Lev Vygotsky's sociocultural theory of learning emphasizes the fundamental role of social interaction in the development of cognition. He believed that learning is a deeply social process. ( ① ) A key concept in his theory is the "Zone of Proximal Development" (ZPD), which is the gap between what a learner can do alone and what they can achieve with guidance. ( ② ) Vygotsky argued that a child first learns new skills and concepts on a social level, through interaction with a more knowledgeable other, such as a parent or teacher. ( ③ ) Then, the child gradually internalizes this learning, transforming it into an individual, internal cognitive process. ( ④ ) For example, a child first learns to solve a puzzle with a parent's help, and eventually, they can solve similar puzzles on their own by using the strategies they learned. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

sociocultural 사회문화적인    proximal 근접한    internalizes 내면화하다    self-regulation 자기 조절

249 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 371-380
[ Passage 373 ]

373. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This shift from a product-centric to a customer-centric model is a hallmark of modern marketing.

The philosophy of marketing has undergone a significant evolution over the past century. In the early 20th century, many businesses operated under a production orientation, assuming that consumers would favor products that were available and highly affordable. ( ① ) The focus was on achieving high production efficiency and wide distribution. ( ② ) Later, a sales orientation became dominant, where companies focused on aggressive selling and promotion to overcome consumer resistance. ( ③ ) However, in the latter half of the 20th century, a new approach emerged: the marketing concept. ( ④ ) It holds that achieving organizational goals depends on knowing the needs and wants of target markets and delivering the desired satisfactions better than competitors do. ( ⑤ ) Instead of "make and sell," the philosophy became "sense and respond."

vera's flavor 어휘

orientation 지향    aggressive 공격적인    hallmark 특징    sense and respond 감지하고 반응하라

[ Passage 374 ]

374. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This is because the very act of observing a quantum system inevitably disturbs it, a fundamental concept known as the observer effect.

Quantum mechanics, the physics of the very small, introduced concepts that radically challenged our classical, everyday intuition about the world. One of the most famous of these is the Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle. ( ① ) It states that there is a fundamental limit to the precision with which certain pairs of physical properties of a particle, such as its position and momentum, can be known simultaneously. ( ② ) In other words, the more precisely one property is measured, the less precisely the other can be known. ( ③ ) For example, to determine an electron's position, a physicist must bounce a photon of light off it, but this interaction necessarily changes the electron's momentum. ( ④ ) Therefore, the uncertainty is not a flaw in our measurement tools but an inherent property of the quantum world itself. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

radically 근본적으로    precision 정밀도    simultaneously 동시에    inherent 내재된

250 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 371-380
[ Passage 375 ]

375. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This process, known as permineralization, essentially turns the bone into a stone replica of its original form.

Fossilization is an exceptionally rare event, requiring a specific set of circumstances to preserve the remains of an organism. ( ① ) The first and most critical step is rapid burial. The organism's body must be quickly covered by sediment, such as sand or mud, to protect it from scavengers and decomposition. ( ② ) Over millions of years, as more layers of sediment accumulate on top, the pressure increases and turns the sediment into rock. ( ③ ) During this time, groundwater rich in dissolved minerals seeps into the porous spaces within the bones. ( ④ ) The minerals gradually crystallize and replace the organic material of the bone. ( ⑤ ) Eventually, geological uplift and erosion may expose the fossil, allowing it to be discovered.

vera's flavor 어휘

circumstances 상황    scavengers 청소 동물    decomposition 부패    permineralization 광물 치환 작용

[ Passage 376 ]

376. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Instead, they sought to use color and form to express their inner emotions and spiritual ideas.

Post-Impressionism was not a single, unified movement but a term encompassing the diverse artistic styles that emerged after Impressionism. ( ① ) While building on the Impressionists' use of vivid color and real-life subject matter, Post-Impressionist artists felt that the focus on capturing the fleeting effects of light was too superficial. ( ② ) They rejected the Impressionist goal of objective, naturalistic representation. ( ③ ) For example, Vincent van Gogh used swirling brushstrokes and intense, non-naturalistic colors to convey his emotional turmoil. ( ④ ) Similarly, Paul Gauguin used flat areas of symbolic color to explore themes of spirituality and primitivism. ( ⑤ ) This shift toward personal expression paved the way for major 20th-century art movements like Expressionism and Cubism.

vera's flavor 어휘

encompassing 포함하는    fleeting 덧없는    superficial 피상적인    turmoil 혼란

251 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 371-380
[ Passage 377 ]

377. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This is because the very act of observing a quantum particle, such as by shining light on it, inevitably alters its state.

Quantum mechanics, the theory describing the behavior of matter and energy at the atomic and subatomic levels, introduced several concepts that defy our everyday intuition. ( ① ) One of the most famous of these is the Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle. ( ② ) It states that there is a fundamental limit to the precision with which we can simultaneously know certain pairs of physical properties, such as a particle's position and its momentum. ( ③ ) In other words, the more precisely you measure a particle's position, the less precisely you can know its momentum, and vice versa. ( ④ ) Therefore, the uncertainty is not a result of flawed measuring instruments but is an inherent property of the quantum world itself. ( ⑤ ) This principle fundamentally changed our understanding of the relationship between the observer and the observed.

vera's flavor 어휘

defy 반항하다, 거부하다    precision 정밀도    momentum 운동량    inherent 내재된

[ Passage 378 ]

378. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This is because the toxin is often fat-soluble and becomes stored in the organism's fatty tissues rather than being excreted.

Bioaccumulation is the process by which certain toxic substances accumulate in the tissues of a living organism over time. ( ① ) This occurs when an organism absorbs a substance at a rate faster than that at which the substance is lost by catabolism and excretion. ( ② ) As a result, the concentration of the toxin within the organism can become much higher than its concentration in the surrounding environment. ( ③ ) For example, small fish might absorb low levels of mercury from the water. ( ④ ) When a larger fish eats many of these smaller fish, the mercury from each of them is transferred and accumulates in the larger fish's body. ( ⑤ ) This process is then magnified at each successive level of the food chain, a phenomenon known as biomagnification.

vera's flavor 어휘

bioaccumulation 생물 축적    catabolism 이화작용    excretion 배설    fat-soluble 지용성의

252 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 371-380
[ Passage 379 ]

379. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

However, this same process can be detrimental when it leads to the formation of harmful stereotypes.

The human brain has a natural tendency to categorize information to make sense of a complex world. This cognitive shortcut allows us to process vast amounts of data efficiently by grouping similar objects, people, or ideas together. ( ① ) For instance, we quickly learn to categorize animals into groups like "mammals," "birds," and "insects," which helps us make rapid judgments and predictions about them. ( ② ) This ability to generalize is a fundamental aspect of learning and survival. ( ③ ) A stereotype is essentially an oversimplified and often inaccurate generalization about a group of people. ( ④ ) Once formed, these stereotypes can be highly resistant to change due to confirmation bias, where we selectively notice information that supports our existing beliefs. ( ⑤ ) This can lead to prejudice and discrimination, demonstrating how a useful mental tool can have damaging social consequences.

vera's flavor 어휘

categorize 분류하다    generalize 일반화하다    detrimental 해로운    prejudice 편견

[ Passage 380 ]

380. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

In contrast, inductive reasoning moves from specific observations to broader generalizations and theories.

In logic and critical thinking, there are two primary methods of reasoning: deduction and induction. Deductive reasoning starts with a general statement or hypothesis and works its way down to a specific conclusion. ( ① ) If the initial premises are true, then the conclusion is guaranteed to be true. ( ② ) For example, if the premise is "All birds have feathers" and "A robin is a bird," the deductive conclusion is "A robin has feathers." ( ③ ) For instance, if you observe that every swan you have ever seen is white, you might induce the general rule that "all swans are white." ( ④ ) This type of reasoning allows for the conclusion to be false even if the premises are true, as it is based on probability rather than certainty. ( ⑤ ) Both forms of reasoning are crucial tools in scientific inquiry and everyday problem-solving.

vera's flavor 어휘

deduction 연역    induction 귀납    premises 전제    probability 확률

253 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 381-390
[ Passage 381 ]

381. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

The second law of thermodynamics introduces a concept called entropy, which is essentially a measure of disorder or randomness in a system.

( ① ) In our daily lives, we observe that things tend to become messy over time. A clean room, if left alone, will accumulate dust and clutter. ( ② ) This tendency is not just a matter of poor housekeeping; it is a fundamental principle of the universe. ( ③ ) For example, a solid ice cube, which has a highly ordered structure, will naturally melt into liquid water, where molecules move more randomly. ( ④ ) This process is a one-way street; a puddle of water will not spontaneously freeze back into an ice cube without external energy being applied. ( ⑤ ) This universal trend towards increasing disorder governs everything from chemical reactions to the ultimate fate of the cosmos.

vera's flavor 어휘

thermodynamics 열역학    entropy 엔트로피    accumulate 축적하다    spontaneously 자발적으로

[ Passage 382 ]

382. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

In essence, this principle suggests that through specialization and trade, all participating countries can achieve a higher level of consumption and economic well-being.

The theory of comparative advantage, developed by economist David Ricardo, is a cornerstone of international trade theory. ( ① ) It argues that countries should specialize in producing goods where they have a lower opportunity cost, even if they have an absolute advantage in producing all goods. ( ② ) For instance, imagine Country A can produce both wine and cloth more efficiently than Country B. ( ③ ) However, if Country A is vastly more efficient at producing wine than cloth, it should focus its resources on wine production. ( ④ ) Country B, in turn, should specialize in cloth, even if it is less efficient in absolute terms. By trading with each other, both nations can consume more of both goods than they could in isolation. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

comparative advantage 비교 우위    opportunity cost 기회비용    vastly 대단히    isolation 고립

254 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 381-390
[ Passage 383 ]

383. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

The Black Death of the mid-14th century was not merely a public health crisis; it was a transformative event that reshaped the social and economic fabric of Europe.

( ① ) The plague wiped out an estimated 30% to 50% of Europe's population, creating a massive demographic shock. ( ② ) This drastic reduction in population led to a severe labor shortage. As a result, surviving peasants and workers could demand higher wages and better working conditions, which significantly weakened the traditional manorial system. ( ③ ) The authority of the Catholic Church, unable to provide explanations or solace, was also profoundly challenged. ( ④ ) Furthermore, the psychological impact on the survivors fostered a new cultural outlook, often characterized by a focus on mortality and earthly life. ( ⑤ ) These deep-seated changes accelerated the decline of feudalism and paved the way for the modern era.

vera's flavor 어휘

transformative 변혁적인    demographic 인구 통계의    manorial system 장원제    solace 위안

[ Passage 384 ]

384. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

However, the concept of free will posits that individuals have the capacity to make choices and decisions that are not predetermined.

Determinism is the philosophical view that all events, including human actions, are ultimately determined by causes external to the will. ( ① ) From this perspective, every event is the necessary result of antecedent events and conditions. ( ② ) A determinist would argue that if one knew all the physical laws of the universe and the initial conditions, one could, in principle, predict the entire future. ( ③ ) This view holds that despite external influences and internal dispositions, humans are the ultimate authors of their own actions. ( ④ ) The debate between these two opposing views has been a central and enduring problem in philosophy. ( ⑤ ) Some philosophers, known as compatibilists, have attempted to reconcile the two, arguing that free will and determinism can coexist.

vera's flavor 어휘

determinism 결정론    antecedent 선행하는    free will 자유의지    reconcile 조화시키다

255 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 381-390
[ Passage 385 ]

385. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Social mobility, the movement of individuals or groups in social position over time, is a key concept in understanding the structure of societies.

( ① ) It allows sociologists to analyze the extent to which a society is open or closed. ( ② ) Sociologists distinguish between different types of this movement. ( ③ ) Vertical mobility refers to a change in social status, either upward or downward, such as a factory worker becoming a CEO or vice versa. ( ④ ) In contrast, horizontal mobility is a change in position at the same social level, like a doctor who moves from one practice to another. ( ⑤ ) These patterns can be studied across generations (intergenerational mobility) or within a person's own career (intragenerational mobility).

vera's flavor 어휘

social mobility 사회 이동    vertical 수직의    horizontal 수평의    intergenerational 세대 간의

[ Passage 386 ]

386. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Therefore, the placebo effect highlights the powerful connection between the mind and the body, demonstrating how belief alone can trigger real physiological changes.

The placebo effect is a fascinating phenomenon where a patient experiences real health improvements from a treatment with no actual therapeutic value. ( ① ) This is commonly seen in clinical trials where one group of participants receives the actual drug while a control group receives a "placebo," such as a sugar pill. ( ② ) Remarkably, a significant portion of the control group often reports a reduction in symptoms. ( ③ ) The mechanism is not fully understood, but it is believed to involve complex neurobiological processes. ( ④ ) For example, the brain may release its own natural painkillers, called endorphins, or expectations of improvement can reduce stress and anxiety, which in turn benefits health. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

placebo effect 플라시보 효과    therapeutic 치료의    neurobiological 신경생물학적인    endorphins 엔도르핀

256 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 381-390
[ Passage 387 ]

387. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

In response to the Great Depression, John Maynard Keynes developed a revolutionary economic theory that challenged the classical view of self-regulating markets.

( ① ) Before Keynes, classical economics held that economies would naturally return to full employment without government intervention. ( ② ) However, the prolonged and severe unemployment of the 1930s contradicted this belief. ( ③ ) Keynes argued that aggregate demand—the total spending in an economy—was the primary determinant of overall economic activity and a cause of unemployment. ( ④ ) He proposed that during economic downturns, governments should actively intervene to manage demand. ( ⑤ ) This could be done through fiscal policy, such as increasing government spending or cutting taxes to stimulate consumption and investment.

vera's flavor 어휘

self-regulating 자기 조절의    prolonged 장기적인    aggregate demand 총수요    fiscal policy 재정 정책

[ Passage 388 ]

388. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

For instance, Martin Luther's Ninety-five Theses were rapidly printed and distributed, fueling the Protestant Reformation far more quickly than would have been possible before.

Johannes Gutenberg's invention of the printing press with movable type in the mid-15th century was a pivotal moment in history. ( ① ) It allowed for the mass production of books and other written materials at a fraction of the cost of manual copying. ( ② ) This technological breakthrough had profound consequences for European society. ( ③ ) One of the most significant impacts was the rapid dissemination of new ideas and knowledge, which broke the monopoly on information previously held by the clergy and nobility. ( ④ ) The increased availability of texts also spurred a rise in literacy rates among the general population, empowering individuals to read and interpret information for themselves. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

movable type 가동 활자    dissemination 보급, 전파    monopoly 독점    spurred 박차를 가했다

257 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 381-390
[ Passage 389 ]

389. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Ultimately, the goal of a Stoic is to achieve a state of inner peace and tranquility by accepting the world as it is and mastering one's own responses to it.

Stoicism, an ancient school of philosophy founded in Athens, offers a practical guide to living a virtuous life. ( ① ) Its central tenet is that virtue is the only true good and that external things like health, wealth, and pleasure are neither good nor bad in themselves. ( ② ) The Stoics taught that we cannot control external events, but we can control our judgments about them. ( ③ ) Therefore, the path to happiness lies in focusing on what is within our power—our thoughts, attitudes, and actions. ( ④ ) By distinguishing between what we can and cannot control, we can avoid being disturbed by misfortune or the actions of others. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

virtuous 덕이 있는    tenet 원칙    tranquility 평온    misfortune 불운

[ Passage 390 ]

390. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

It can lead to poor decision-making, reinforce harmful stereotypes, and make it difficult for individuals to change their minds even when presented with compelling evidence.

Confirmation bias is the cognitive tendency to search for, interpret, favor, and recall information in a way that confirms or supports one's preexisting beliefs. ( ① ) This mental shortcut affects how we gather information and how we interpret and remember it. ( ② ) For example, a person who believes that left-handed people are more creative than right-handed people will be more likely to notice and remember instances of creative left-handers, while ignoring or forgetting counterexamples. ( ③ ) This bias is not necessarily intentional; it often operates at an unconscious level as the brain tries to simplify information processing. ( ④ ) However, it can have serious negative consequences. ( ⑤ )

vera's flavor 어휘

confirmation bias 확증 편향    preexisting 기존의    counterexamples 반례    compelling 설득력 있는

258 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 391-400
[ Passage 391 ]

391. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This allows for real-time feedback, precise targeting based on demographics and user behavior, and detailed analytics to gauge the success of a campaign.

The world of marketing can be broadly divided into two distinct categories: traditional and digital. Traditional marketing refers to any type of promotion or advertising that has been in use by companies for years, long before the internet became a household utility. ( ① ) It includes methods such as print advertisements in newspapers and magazines, broadcast commercials on television and radio, and direct mail. ( ② ) The primary characteristic of this approach is its wide, and often untargeted, reach, casting a broad net to capture as many potential customers as possible. ( ③ ) In contrast, digital marketing leverages online platforms to connect with a specific audience in a more interactive and measurable way. ( ④ ) For instance, social media marketing and search engine optimization (SEO) are powerful tools within this domain. ( ⑤ ) While both approaches aim to attract customers, they operate on fundamentally different principles and utilize different channels.

vera's flavor 어휘

leverages 활용하다    demographics 인구 통계    gauge 측정하다    domain 영역

[ Passage 392 ]

392. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Under this system, citizens elect officials—such as presidents, prime ministers, and members of parliament—to represent their interests and make decisions.

Democracy, a system of government by the whole population, can be implemented in different ways. The two primary forms are direct democracy and representative democracy. In a direct democracy, citizens themselves vote on laws and policies directly, rather than electing officials to make decisions on their behalf. ( ① ) This model was famously practiced in ancient Athens, where eligible citizens could speak and vote in the assembly. ( ② ) While it represents the purest form of popular rule, it can be cumbersome and inefficient, especially as the population grows. ( ③ ) On the other hand, representative democracy is often considered more practical for large, modern nations where such direct participation is unfeasible. ( ④ ) These elected officials are accountable to the people and can be replaced in subsequent elections. ( ⑤ ) Thus, the choice between these systems often involves a trade-off between direct citizen control and governmental efficiency.

vera's flavor 어휘

implemented 시행된    cumbersome 번거로운    unfeasible 실행 불가능한    accountable 책임이 있는

259 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 391-400
[ Passage 393 ]

393. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

Music became a medium for conveying intense feelings, dramatic narratives, and the inner world of the composer.

The transition from the Classical to the Romantic era in Western music represents a significant shift in artistic philosophy. The Classical period, spanning roughly from 1750 to 1820, is characterized by its emphasis on clarity, balance, and structural order. ( ① ) Composers like Haydn and Mozart are prime examples, creating works that are elegant, restrained, and formally precise. ( ② ) Their music often follows predictable patterns and structures, such as the sonata form, aiming for universal beauty rather than personal confession. ( ③ ) Conversely, the Romantic era that followed prioritized emotional expression and individual subjectivity above all else. ( ④ ) Composers such as Beethoven, Schubert, and Chopin broke free from the rigid constraints of the earlier period, exploring new harmonies, dynamic ranges, and longer, more complex forms. ( ⑤ ) This fundamental change in focus redefined the purpose and potential of music for generations to come.

vera's flavor 어휘

spanning ~에 걸친    restrained 절제된    subjectivity 주관성    constraints 제약

[ Passage 394 ]

394. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

It overlays computer-generated images, sounds, and information onto the real world in real-time.

Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR) are two transformative technologies that are often confused, but they offer fundamentally different experiences. VR aims to create a completely immersive, artificial world that replaces the user's real-world surroundings. ( ① ) Users typically wear a headset that blocks out external stimuli, transporting them to a computer-generated environment, be it a fantasy landscape or a simulated training ground. ( ② ) This total immersion is the defining feature of the VR experience. ( ③ ) Augmented Reality (AR), however, operates on a different principle by enhancing the user's existing environment rather than replacing it. ( ④ ) A well-known example is the mobile game Pokémon GO, where players see digital creatures in their actual physical surroundings through their phone's camera. ( ⑤ ) In essence, VR creates a new reality, while AR alters the one we already perceive.

vera's flavor 어휘

immersive 몰입형의    stimuli 자극(복수형)    overlays 겹쳐 놓다    alters 바꾸다

260 | vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편
vera's flavor 편입영어 독해 bridge편 Part 4: 문장삽입 | 지문 391-400
[ Passage 395 ]

395. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

In this type of interaction, one organism, the parasite, benefits at the expense of the other, the host, which is harmed.

Symbiosis describes a close and long-term interaction between two different biological species. These relationships can be categorized based on the outcome for each participant. ( ① ) One of the most well-known types is mutualism, in which both species benefit from the interaction. ( ② ) For example, bees get nectar from flowers for food, and in the process, they pollinate the flowers, allowing them to reproduce. ( ③ ) A starkly different form of symbiosis is parasitism, where the relationship is not mutually beneficial. ( ④ ) A classic example is a tick feeding on the blood of a dog; the tick gains nourishment while the dog suffers from blood loss and potential disease transmission. ( ⑤ ) Understanding these different symbiotic relationships is crucial for comprehending the complex web of life in any ecosystem.

vera's flavor 어휘

symbiosis 공생    mutualism 상리공생    parasitism 기생    at the expense of ~을 희생하여

[ Passage 396 ]

396. 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

[주어진 문장]

This approach seeks to gain an in-depth understanding of human behavior and the reasons that govern such behavior.

In academic and scientific fields, research methodologies are often categorized as either quantitative or qualitative. Quantitative research is concerned with collecting and analyzing numerical data to identify patterns, make predictions, and test causal relationships. ( ① ) It typically involves large sample sizes and statistical analysis to ensure that the findings are generalizable to a larger population. ( ② ) The goal is often to quantify a problem and understand its prevalence by seeking objective, measurable evidence.
